AAT

Architecture Aptitude Test



About the exam-







Only students who wish to enrol in the B.Arch program provided by IIT Kharagpur and IIT Roorkee must take the Architecture Aptitude Test (AAT). Only those students will get the opportunity to take this aptitude test for admission who have passed JEE Main and JEE Advanced.

Eligibility criteria for this exam-



The qualifying requirements established by the institutes for the B.Arch program must be met by applicants. Anyone who does not fulfil one of the eligibility requirements will be excluded. The prerequisites are as follows:

Education qualification - 

Candidates must have been eligible to take the JEE Advanced 2022 test.

The applicant's performance in class XII will be taken into account. See the IITs' performance standards for further information.

Age limit -

Candidates who have completed 16 years of age are eligible to apply. There is no upper age limit for candidates.

Exam Application process -



STEPS FOR FILLING OUT THE APPLICATION FORM-



Step 1 - Check out the official webpage.

Step 2 -To access the AAT application form, click the link.

Step 3 -Enter your date of birth, email address, mobile number, and JEE Advanced 2022 registration number.

Step 4 -You'll be questioned about your desire in taking the AAT 2022 exam. Choose "Yes" from the menu.

Step 5 -The list of exam locations will appear. Choose what you prefer.

Step 6 -Include all the necessary information.

Step 7 -Before submitting the application form, please review all the information.

Step 8 -The additional application cost for the AAT exam is not required of candidates. There will be a JEE Advanced application fee.





And selection procedure of the exam-



The AAT cut-off will be decided by the Joint Implementation Committee of JEE Advanced 2022. The cut-off will serve as an exam screening method. There won't be a specific cut-off announced for each category. The cut-off will be established depending on a number of variables, including the cut-off from the previous year, the exam's level of difficulty, the number of applications, etc.



The AAT 2022 seat allocation procedure will be managed by the Joint Seat Allocation Authority (JoSAA). Candidates must register online in order to participate in the seat allocation procedure. Based on the rank, preference, category, and availability of seats, the seat will be assigned. Candidates must visit the assigned institute for document verification and admission requirements after being assigned a seat.



A number of modifications were made last year, including the elimination of a cut-off for class 12 marks and the addition of one fewer round of counselling, among other things. To ensure prompt admissions and the start of classes, the number of counselling sessions has been decreased from seven to six.

BORDER SECURITY FORCE(BSF)



About the exam-







The Union Public Service Commission recruits Assistant Commandants for the Border Security Force (BSF) (UPSC). One of the most coveted and sought-after positions in the BSF, the exam is held at the national level.



Applicants for the position of Assistant Commandant in the BSF must pass a standardised exam for the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF). Every year, the Assistant Commandant test is held by UPSC, and the commission chooses the number of openings based on the requirements and needs.

Eligibility criteria for this exam-



The official notification contains the eligibility requirements for the position of BSF Assistant Commandant, and each applicant must meet these requirements in order to submit an application for the exam.

The BSF eligibility requirements comprise:



Nationality:

The applicant must be an Indian citizen, a subject of Nepal, or a citizen of Bhutan.

Candidates of either gender may apply.

Age:

The ideal candidate will be between 20 and 25 years old.

 However, applicants from the SC, ST, and Persons with Disabilities groups are given age breaks.

Educational Qualification:

The candidate must hold a bachelor's degree from one of the institutions listed in the state or federal government's act. Alternatively, the applicant must hold a bachelor's degree from a deemed university.

The application must be filed online by the candidates. No other method of application submission will be permitted. The methods below can be used by applicants to complete the exam application form.

Step 1 - Candidates should visit rectt.bsf.gov.in, the BSF's official website.



Step 2 - Click on the BSF Constable GD Apply Online link after that.

Step 3 - Fill out the "ONE TIME REGISTRATION" (OTR) form with the necessary information about them, including Name, Mobile Number, and Email Address. Candidates should make sure that only functional, active cell numbers and email addresses are utilised for one-time registration.



Step 4 -Candidates will receive their Registration ID and password on their provided mobile number and email address after submitting their information.

Candidates should properly fill out the appropriate parts of the online application form and upload all required documents after carefully reading the instructions.

It is not necessary to send printouts of applications to BSF.

Step 2 - Those candidates who have been shortlisted on the basis of the result will be now writing the mains exam. 

Step 3 - An interview round will be conducted for those shortlisted candidates who have successfully passed both the preliminary exam round and mains exam round.

(Chartered Financial Analyst )







About the exam-



 The US-based CFA Institute's professional curriculum is called "Chartered Financial Analyst." The program's mission is to help investment and financial professionals advance their careers by awarding them a "CFA charter." The graduate-level CFA program has three test levels: I, II, and III. The computer-based test modality will be used to administer the CFA exam. In India, the exam for the June session is given in 10 cities, while the exam for the December session is given in 6 cities. A wide range of financial topics, such as investment management, financial analysis, stocks, bonds, and derivatives, are covered in the program. Each level of the CFA exam has a different format and degree of complexity.

Eligibility criteria for this exam-



The following list of requirements is for CFA first level eligibility:



The applicant needs to have a passport for foreign travel.



Additionally, the candidate must meet one of the criteria below:



Undergraduate



The applicant must be a recent graduate or a bachelor's degree candidate in their last year. (The candidate must have finished a bachelor's degree program in order to register for the Level II exam.) or



Work history:



The candidate must have four years of work experience or a minimum of four years of work experience and education.





Paper pattern & marking scheme of the examination -







LEVEL I - 



180 multiple-choice questions from the Level I test are given in two 135-minute sessions (session times are approximate). The interval between sessions is optional. An exam result can only be obtained if candidates attend both sessions.



90 multiple-choice questions covering economics, ethics and professional standards, mathematical methodologies, and financial statement analysis were presented in the first session, which lasted two hours and fifteen minutes.



90 multiple-choice questions covering corporate issuers, stock, fixed income, derivatives, alternative investments, and portfolio management were presented in the second session (2 hours, 15 minutes).



LEVEL II - 



The CFA Level II exam is composed of 22 item sets that include vignettes and 88 related multiple-choice questions.



The Level II test will take place over the course of four and a half hours, divided into two equal periods of two hours and twelve minutes each, with a possible break in between.



Candidates may require proctor help at any point during the exam's normal course. Proctors will arrive at workstations as soon as they can to offer assistance. If possible, we advise candidates to finish their exam before a proctor arrives. To ensure that candidates still have enough time to answer all questions, our exam writers constructed the test to accommodate for brief stops or interruptions.

Sales tax, where applicable, will be charged to the fees displayed during the payment/checkout process where CFA Institute is obligated to do so at the applicable local statutory rate.



Step 3 -When you register for the Level I exam and the CFA Program, there is a one-time enrollment fee. The first registration price will drop to USD 350 for 2023 from USD 450. The succeeding exams will not be charged this price. You must pay all fees in US dollars.

There are three levels to the course:



Level I: Emphasis on fundamental understanding of ten subject areas and easy investing tool analysis



Level II: Places a strong emphasis on using concepts and investment techniques, with a particular attention to valuing all THE assets.



Focus on using all the strategies and ideas learnt for efficient portfolio management and wealth planning at Level III.



Each level's exam will last six hours, and a six-month preparation period is advised.



Each level's application procedure is disclosed six months in advance. The candidates can sign up for the level of their choice, study for the qualifying exam during the following six months, and then take the test.

About the exam-







A professional qualification in management accounting and financial management is called Certified Management Accountant (CMA). The qualification denotes that the holder has expertise in financial analysis, planning, control, decision-making, and professional ethics. There are numerous professional organisations with management accounting credentials all throughout the world. The primary organisations that provide CMA certification are:



Institute of Management Accountants USA;

Eligibility criteria for this exam-





As previously established, there are three stages to the CMA program: foundation, intermediate, and final. When a student passes all three levels of the exam, they are given the CMA credential. For each level of the course, there are different requirements to take the CMA exam. The applicants must review the requirements listed on the ICAI's official website. Only individuals who meet these requirements are permitted to take the tests.



Education Requirements-

A bachelor's degree from a college or university with accreditation.

A candidate must have completed class 10+2 or the equivalent from a recognized educational institution in order to sit for the CMA foundation test.

If a bachelor's degree is not offered, one alternative is to take the exam and then complete the bachelor's degree program within seven years following the exam.

Candidates must pass the foundation exam in order to sit for the CMA intermediate level exam, and they must pass the intermediate exam in order to sit for the CMA final exam

Since there are three tests for the CMA courses, each exam's exam format is unique, but the fundamental structure is the same. The foundation course test has questions of an objective nature, whereas the intermediate and final course exams contain questions of a descriptive and numerical nature.



There are a total of four exams for the foundation course, and the question paper is made up of multiple-choice questions. There are two groups of four examinations each in the intermediate and final courses, and there are questions of the descriptive and numerical types on the exam paper.



Exam Mode: The CMA tests are computer-based, and if the candidate is taking the test from home, proctors will be present.The exam lasts for three hours, plus an additional fifteen minutes for reading.



Each exam paper carries 100 total points.

NATIONAL APTITUDE TEST IN ARCHITECTURE



About the exam-







The National Institute of Advanced Studies in Architecture (NIASA), a division of the Council of Architecture, New Delhi, India, administers the National Aptitude Test in Architecture (NATA). For admission to undergraduate architecture programs, candidates must pass the National Aptitude Test in Architecture.

The Council of Architecture has established NATA or the JEE Mains Paper 2 test as a requirement for enrollment in any architectural college or program in India.





Overview/Highlights of this exam-



Particulars

Details

Name of the examination

National Aptitude Test in Architecture

Exam conducting authority

 National Institute of Advanced Studies in Architecture (NIASA)

Frequency of the exam

Courses offered through the exam

B.Arch degree course

Examination level 

National

Medium of examination 

English

Mode of the examination

pen-paper based

Exam official website

nata.in



Eligibility criteria for this exam-

Education Qualification-



Candidates who have passed the 10+2 exam or are attempting it, with at least 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Maths, as well as 50% overall, or who have passed or are attempting the 10+3 Diploma exam with Maths as a required subject, with at least 50% overall. 

A 5 percent mark reduction is given to students in the SC/ST, OBC-NCL, and PwD categories for PCM, 10+2 level, and 10+3 diploma levels.



Paper pattern & marking scheme of the examination -





Section

Name of the subjects

Section A

Cognitive Skills for Drawing and Visual



Composition Test 

Section B

Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics



General Aptitude and Logical Reasoning 





The National Aptitude Test in Architecture (NATA) is administered at the national level by the Council of Architecture for admission to the five-year B.Arch. degree program at all accredited universities in India. 

The admissions committees of several Government, Government Aided, and Unaided schools and colleges of architecture use NATA scores.

The test gauges one's ability to draw and observe, as well as their sense of proportion, aesthetic awareness, and critical thinking skills.

The test is divided into two sections: a drawing test on paper is part of the first section, and an online test with multiple-choice questions about physics, chemistry, maths, and general aptitude is part of the second section.

Twice a year, the entire nation takes the one-day Nata exam.

The Nata test lasts three hours and has a total score of 200.



Available seats & participating institutions-



NATA is the qualifying exam for admission to Architecture courses across India. Some NATA Participating Colleges or Institutions are:

Registration for PTU B.Pharmacy 2022 will begin in August of that year. I. K. Exam Application process -



STEPS- 



Visit the organisation's website.

Check out the website's terms and conditions for the admissions process and counselling process.

Register on the official website to complete the registration process.

One must provide information upon registration, including the student's name, email address, and valid mobile number, as well as carefully and accurately creating a password on the website.

The credentials (username and password) will be issued to the apprentice's mobile number or email address after registration.

Use the login information to access the website and download the PTU B.Pharmacy First Year Application Form for 2022.

Fill out the PTU B.Pharmacy 1st year 2022 Application Form with the necessary information, including personal (name, email address, phone number, and address), educational (passed examination, board and institution name, passing year), academic (academic data), and category (category details).

And selection procedure of the exam-



To grant admission to the apprentice in the B.Pharmacy 1st year Course offered in various institutions and colleges, the PTU B.Pharmacy 1st year 2022 Admission Procedure entails a Centralised Counselling process.

The application form and counselling form are both available on the official website and must be filled out and submitted by apprentices. Based on the various national level scorecards, the authorities will examine the application forms and suggest a few apprentices.

The Amrit list and rank list will be created by the authorities using a distinct national level scorecard. The apprentice will be admitted to the first-year B.Pharmacy courses at I. K. Gujral Punjab Technical University, Jalandhar, depending on the rank list, their choice, rank, and the availability of seats.

Students who are interested in the positions of technicians and assistant loco pilots take the RRB Assistant Loco Pilot Test, also known as the RRB ALP exam. It is given across all of India's railway zones and is managed by the Railway Recruitment Board.

The country is home to numerous Railway Recruitment Boards. To select qualified candidates for each of the 21 Railway Recruitment Boards, the RRB ALP exam is held. STEP 1 - Candidates must first visit the Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University's official website.



 STEP 2 - The link to an application form should be available on the official website for the applicants to click on.

The abbreviation for the Uttar Pradesh State Entrance Exam is UPSEE. Students who want to enrol in different colleges connected with Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University, Lucknow, must pass this test at the state level. Those candidates who have successfully passed the UPSEE can further participate in the B.Arch, B.Pharm, hotel management, MCA, MBA, MAM, and fashion and apparel design courses.

Eligibility criteria for this exam-



Education qualification-

The candidate must have passed the intermediate test administered by the Uttar Pradesh Board or the Class 12 exam, or a course of study equivalent, administered by any recognized Board.

Candidates must have earned passing grades in all mandatory courses and one optional topic on their 12th-grade exams without receiving any grace points.

Minimum passing percentage for the general category is 45% in all the core subjects such as Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. 

Candidates belonging to the reserved category, the minimum passing percentage is 40.

Only those applicants who have successfully completed a three-year architecture diploma (10+3) recognized by the federal or state governments are eligible for admission to the first year of the B.Arch. They can only enrol once they have earned a diploma with a minimum of 50% overall without using any sort of grace.

Additionally, they must take Paper 4 of the admission exam for the first year of the B.Arch.

Please keep in mind that JEE Main results will be used to determine admission to B.Tech programs from candidates who wish to apply.

And selection procedure of the exam-



After the registration period for counselling, Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University, Lucknow, will release the UPSEE 2022 merit list. Students will be shortlisted on the basis of the merit list which is created from the results of JEE Mains. The UPSEE 2022 counselling session will be open to candidates whose names appear on the merit list. Based on the counselling, they will be assigned seats. If any two students received the same score on the JEE Mains, the division will be done as follows:



Candidates will be ranked higher if they give less incorrect answers.

In the event of a tie, the applicant who had the highest overall PCM score will be given the opportunity first.

In case any two candidates receive the same position, then the preference will be given to the candidate who is older in age.

WBJEE

West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination 



About the exam-







The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board administers the West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination, also known as the WBJEE, which is a state-controlled, centralised test for admission to numerous private and public engineering institutes in West Bengal. 



After completing grade 12, students take the test to be admitted to graduation (also known as bachelor's) courses. Anyone who completed the physics, chemistry, and mathematics courses in the pure science and vocational 10+2 streams at the plus two level is eligible to sit the exam.

pen-paper based

wbjeeb.in/wbjee



Eligibility criteria for this exam-



NATIONALITY



The candidate needs to be an Indian national.



AGE REQUIREMENTS

To sit for the exam, a candidate must be at least 17 years old (as of December 31, 2022) or have been born on or before that day. There is no maximum age for taking the exam.



However, as of December 31, 2022, the minimum age requirement for enrollment to the Marine Engineering Course is 17 years old, and the maximum age requirement is 25 years old.

Category I: One mark will be given for each accurate response. A wrong answer results in a deduction of one-fourth of a mark. Any combination of responses that include more than one option will be deemed wrong and receive a score of -1/4 (25% negative).



Category II: A correct response earns two marks but only one option is acceptable. A wrong answer will receive -1/2 (25% negative) marks. Any combination of responses that include more than one option will be deemed wrong and receive a score of -1/2 (25 percent negative).



Category III: A few selections might be accurate. Only accurate answers will earn you two (two) marks. There are no failing grades.

Exam Application process -

Register for the WBJEE online in 2022. Candidates must first complete the WBJEE registration process for 2022 by following the instructions provided: 



 STEP 1 - Visit the WBJEE 2022 official website at wbjee.nic.in.



STEP 2 - Select the link for "New Registration."



STEP 3- Enter information such as your name, your parents' names, your birthdate, your gender, the sort of identification you have, and your phone number.



STEP 4 - additional contact information (address, district, state, PIN code, email ID, mobile number, etc.)



 STEP 5 - With the response, create a password and security question.



STEP 6- Click "Submit" after reviewing the information you gave during WBJEE registration.



And selection procedure of the exam-



Candidates will be chosen based on how well they did on the entrance exam. Students who pass the exam should register for online counselling sessions and pay the counselling cost at Allahabad bank using an e-challan.
